install_tell: git clone https://github.com/h2oai/h2ogpt && cd h2ogpt && docker-compose up -d --build   # UI at https://localhost:7860 ; OpenAI proxy at https://localhost:5000/v1 ; alternate: docker run ... -p 7860:7860 -p 5000:5000 gcr.io/.../h2ogpt python generate.py
misconfig_patterns: Quickstart docker-compose exposed directly to the internet with no reverse-proxy auth and no --auth flag = fully open chat UI
OpenAI proxy on 5000 left unauthenticated (--enforce_h2ogpt_api_key unset) = open LLM gateway / LLM-jacking surface
Upstream LLM provider API key baked into env or --inference_server and reachable through an open proxy
Private/PHI documents ingested into the RAG corpus on an internet-exposed instance
Outdated bundled Gradio version vulnerable to path-traversal file read
--auth_access='open' leaving self-service account creation enabled when auth IS turned on

response_markers: "version" gradio field with h2oGPT component tree (markdown/chatbot/dataframe blocks)
/v1/models returns OpenAI-style {"object":"list","data":[...]} with an h2oai-served model id when probing port 5000
active_probe.false_positive_check: h2oGPT is a Gradio app, so a bare `http.component:"Gradio"` or port-7860 match collides with tens of thousands of unrelated HuggingFace/Gradio demos — that is the ~50% false-positive trap. Disambiguate by requiring the vendor-unique string "h2oGPT"/"h2ogpt" in the title or body, OR by confirming the GET /config JSON contains title "h2oGPT" with the h2oGPT component tree. The paired OpenAI proxy on 5000 (/v1/models answering alongside a Gradio /config on 7860 on the same host) is a strong second discriminator since vanilla Gradio demos do not ship that proxy. Do NOT attribute h2o-3 (the AutoML/Flow product on port 54321 with /3/ and /99/ REST paths) to h2oGPT — different product, different CVEs, different port; the shared "h2oai" string is not sufficient.

deployment_tells: Two-port pairing on a single host: Gradio UI on 7860 + OpenAI-compatible proxy on 5000 (--openai_server defaults on)
Gradio framework banner/headers with an h2oGPT-titled /config
gcr.io / h2oai container image lineage (gcr.io/.../h2ogpt) in image metadata or compose build context
Named volumes cache:/workspace/.cache and save:/workspace/save; auth persisted at /workspace/h2ogpt_auth/auth.db when --auth is enabled
Self-host with local GPU (nvidia deploy reservation in docker-compose) — common on bare-metal/colo, university, and cloud-GPU IPs
pivot_paths: GET /config (port 7860) — read the Gradio app config to confirm h2oGPT and enumerate component/endpoint surface without sending any chat
GET /v1/models (port 5000) — confirm the OpenAI proxy is unauth and enumerate the backing model id (read-only, no completion)
GET /file=<path> — Gradio file route; on unpatched Gradio (CVE-2024-1561) read local config to confirm exposure (restraint: confirm reachability, do not exfiltrate)
List the document collections / chat sources in the UI to characterize whether ingested RAG corpus is private PII vs public docs — names are the finding, no document download
Inspect /info or the OpenAI proxy /v1/models response to attribute the upstream inference backend (vLLM/TGI/OpenAI) for operator attribution
vulnerabilities: CVE-2024-1561 (GHSA-g9cj-cfpp-4g2x) — Gradio path traversal via the local file route; h2oGPT is built on Gradio and exposes /file=, so unpatched Gradio versions allow arbitrary local file read on a default h2oGPT deploy. Affects Gradio < 4.13 (fix in 4.13.0).

No widely-published DEDICATED h2oai/h2ogpt CVE found as of 2026-06; the document-chat app's exposure is dominated by (a) its open-by-default posture and (b) inherited Gradio CVEs rather than a unique app CVE.

ATTRIBUTION CAUTION — the h2oai *AutoML* product h2o-3 (separate codebase, port 54321) carries critical CVEs (CVE-2024-8616 arbitrary file overwrite, CVE-2024-6863 arbitrary file encryption, CVE-2024-6573 / CVE-2025-6544 JDBC deserialization RCE, CVE-2024-7768 / CVE-2024-8062 DoS). These are NOT h2oGPT and must not be cross-attributed; listed only to prevent false fingerprint mapping.
auth_evidence: Default docker-compose.yml (github.com/h2oai/h2ogpt/blob/main/docker-compose.yml) publishes `'${H2OGPT_PORT}:7860'` and runs `command: ${H2OGPT_ARGS}` with NO --auth flag baked in; the documented launch (`docker-compose up -d --build`, reachable at localhost:7860) yields an unauth Gradio UI. docs/FAQ.md states verbatim: "No landing page authentication, but login possible inside app for Login tab" — auth is an opt-in Login tab, not a gate. The documented `docker run` in docs/README_DOCKER.md only adds auth if the operator keeps the optional `--auth=...`/`--h2ogpt_api_keys=...` lines ("for open-access remove --h2ogpt_api_keys line"). Auth flags (--auth, --auth_filename, --enforce_h2ogpt_api_key, --enforce_h2ogpt_ui_key) all default to off/None. Result: quickstart on an exposed port = fully unauth chat UI + unauth OpenAI proxy.
sensitive_assets: Ingested RAG documents / private user-uploaded files (the entire point of h2oGPT is private-doc chat) readable via the doc-collection UI and Gradio /file= path
Upstream LLM API keys (OPENAI_API_KEY / vendor keys) passed via env or --inference_server config, exposed in /config or settings if the proxy/UI leaks them
Conversation history and saved chat state (the /workspace/save volume; --auth_filename auth.db persisted across restarts)
Document collection / vector store contents (Chroma DB built from ingested docs) browsable through the UI
OpenAI-compatible proxy (port 5000) usable as a free unauthenticated LLM gateway = LLM-jacking of the operator's backend model/credits
Model and inference-server backend identity revealed via /v1/models and --inference_server config (vLLM/TGI endpoints)
sources: [CANDIDATE - NuClide Cat-34 rag-app-builders Stage -1 OSINT 2026-06-12; software-default characterization, no live host probed yet]
